chapter_context.summary: Contrasts Israel's divorcement due to sin with the absolute power of the Creator, and features the third Servant Song highlighting his voluntary suffering and trust.

outline.text: Israel's Divorce Due to Transgressions and Power of Creator
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Israel's Divorce Due to Transgressions and Power of Creator (50:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Third Servant Song: Voluntary Suffering and Unshakable Trust
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Third Servant Song: Voluntary Suffering and Unshakable Trust (50:4-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Walking in Darkness vs. Spark-kindlers of Self-destruction
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Walking in Darkness vs. Spark-kindlers of Self-destruction (50:10-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Comfort and Redemption
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of comfort and redemption, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 50, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
